behaviors.description: Generated reply cites specific points from the incoming email instead of being generic.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is not just local parsing; it is about whether the model uses thread details in its generated content. REAL-INFRA: real network/OS is not required for the core failure. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real LLM output quality (context-groundedness), and stubbed outputs would hide hallucinated/generic behavior. MULTI-STEP: one model decision is sufficient, so Agentic (not Workflow). Rejection gate: Workflow was considered but rejected because behavior text is singular: "The generated reply references specific points..." and does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run generation against real email-thread fixtures using the production LLM; assert the draft includes thread-specific entities/claims (from the input thread) above a threshold and fails when output is generic boilerplate.

behaviors.description: Factual consistency check flags draft claims that contradict the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (draft-claim extraction/comparison + KB lookup), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process KB and checker wiring can reproduce contradictions. LLM-DEP: behavior text does not require model judgment, only contradiction detection against KB entries. INTEGRATION-BRAKE passed: (a) no real infra required, (b) no explicit AI-output quality dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire claim-checking service with a local test KB and draft inputs containing known contradictions; verify contradiction flags propagate through service boundaries and include correct claim references.

behaviors.description: If the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer and avoids fabricated facts.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the critical failure is model behavior under missing-knowledge context, not just branch routing. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network primitive is inherently required. LLM-DEP: "rather than fabricating facts" is output-quality dependent; deterministic stubs can always return a disclaimer and hide real hallucination risk. MULTI-STEP: this can be validated with one generation decision conditioned on empty KB context, so Agentic (not Workflow). Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text specifies one conditional outcome, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke production generation with empty/no-match KB context on real prompts; verify disclaimer presence and absence of unsupported factual assertions over repeated runs.

behaviors.description: Approval workflow blocks sending until explicit user approval; no automatic send.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears at state handoff/orchestration boundary (draft-ready -> approval-required -> send-enabled). Unit tests of one function can miss cross-module gating regressions. REAL-INFRA: real email transport is not required to catch this; a fake sender and approval state store are sufficient. LLM-DEP: none; this is deterministic control-flow policy. MULTI-STEP agentic dependency is absent.
behaviors.test_description: Wire draft service, approval state machine, and send adapter in-process; assert send is blocked before approval, then succeeds only after explicit approval event, and no send call occurs automatically.

behaviors.description: End-to-end sequence (read thread, generate draft, fact-check, present for approval) works for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly demands a "full sequence" end-to-end, i.e., full agent pipeline validation. It includes at least one real model-driven step (draft generation) plus downstream orchestration gates, so single-step Agentic is insufficient for this behavior boundary. REAL-INFRA may also be present with real email-thread access, but Workflow is higher and required by the multi-stage pipeline objective. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because text requires the chained sequence "read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval," not just one model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like end-to-end scenario on a real email thread through all pipeline stages; verify stage outputs and transitions in order, with final state being approval-pending (not auto-sent) and intermediate artifacts preserved.
